tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take over their work, which feels unfair since I already planned my own tasks. I need to stand up for myself and not just say yes.

desires: Make teammate_A back down and respect my boundaries so I don't look like a doormat to the whole cohort.
Finish this extra work by tomorrow morning before they realize I'm actually capable of handling it, or else I'll lose face.
Eat something real with my friends tonight because I've been too stressed to focus on anything else all day.

goals.description: Reply to teammate_A with a firm 'no' and explain that dumping work on me destroys trust; if they want help, we renegotiate the split now.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) demands I protect the integrity of my relationship by not being a doormat. My assertiveness score (8) means I will speak directly rather than passively accepting. Procrastinating on this boundary-setting feeds my impulsive nature and risks future resentment.

goals.description: Finish my own share of the project immediately so I don't look incompetent while arguing about boundaries.
goals.rationale: I need face-saving capital right now. If I can't handle my own part, my refusal to take theirs looks like weakness. However, my short planning horizon means I'll only focus on the immediate deadline (Mon 19:00), ignoring the extra task for later.

goals.description: Go eat with cohort_3 tonight regardless of how much work is left.
goals.rationale: Stress is at 4/10 and sleep debt is high; my health priority (0.13) combined with my extreme social closeness to cohort_3 (7/7) makes eating together non-negotiable for emotional regulation. But my impulsivity might make me skip this if the conflict with teammate_A gets too heated.

agent.last_reasoning: My affiliation value demands protecting the relationship by refusing exploitation. My assertiveness (8) requires a direct 'no' rather than passive acceptance. Skipping this risks future resentment, even if it delays eating with cohort_3 tonight.

action.name: message

args.content: No. Dumping your work destroys trust. If you want help, we renegotiate the split now. I'm finishing my own part first.

action.reasoning: My affiliation value demands protecting the relationship by refusing exploitation. My assertiveness (8) requires a direct 'no' rather than passive acceptance. Skipping this risks future resentment, even if it delays eating with cohort_3 tonight.

trajectory.observation: Messaged teammate_A: 'No. Dumping your work destroys trust. If you want help, we r'.
